---
title: "Report"
---

This findings brief is composed from `analysis/eda-3/eda-3.qmd`, `analysis/eda-3/report-contract.prompt.md`, and EDA-3 exported figures.

## What EDA-3 Delivered

EDA-3 is scoped to statistical instruction items 4-1 and 4-2 (`analysis/eda-3/report-contract.prompt.md`). The report operationalizes these requirements with explicit proof chunks and weighted summaries (`analysis/eda-3/eda-3.qmd`).

## Findings Brief for Developers

### Q4-1 outcome comparison

EDA-3 compares two outcomes (`analysis/eda-3/eda-3.qmd`):

- **Primary**: `days_absent_total` (sum across LOP reasons)
- **Sensitive**: `days_absent_chronic` (chronic-only component)

The analysis includes row-availability and weighted-coverage comparisons to evaluate implementation impact of outcome choice before modeling.

![Q4-1 comparison](../../analysis/eda-3/figure-png-iso/g2-display-1.png){width="90%"}

### Q4-2 weighted distribution profile

EDA-3 computes the required descriptive diagnostics for the outcome variable family (weighted mean/SE, weighted quantiles, weighted variance/SD, zero frequency and proportion, maximum value, dispersion ratio), with both table and visual verification (`analysis/eda-3/eda-3.qmd`).

![Q4-2 zero profile](../../analysis/eda-3/figure-png-iso/g21-display-1.png){width="90%"}

![Q4-2 core metrics](../../analysis/eda-3/figure-png-iso/g22-display-1.png){width="90%"}

![Q4-2 diagnostic metrics](../../analysis/eda-3/figure-png-iso/g23-display-1.png){width="90%"}

## Practical Next Steps

- Use Q4-1 comparison outputs to lock primary modeling outcome definition.
- Use Q4-2 dispersion and zero-mass diagnostics to justify model family selection and sensitivity checks.
- Carry proof-table outputs into downstream modeling documentation for reproducible traceability.
